summary: In the introduction to 'The Second Sex', Simone de Beauvoir establishes her foundational thesis that throughout history, man has positioned himself as the essential 'Subject' and woman as the inessential 'Other'. She critiques biological, nominalist, and patriarchal justifications for women's subjugation, arguing from an existentialist perspective that women must be understood through their concrete situations and their struggle for liberty and transcendence, rather than a vague notion of happiness.

principles.statement: Woman is defined and differentiated with reference to man, not as an autonomous being.
principles.rationale: Society treats the masculine as the absolute human type. Man represents both the positive and the neutral, while woman represents only the negative, defined by limiting criteria without reciprocity.
principles.source_quote: He is the Subject, he is the Absolute – she is the Other.

principles.statement: Denying the concrete reality of categories like 'woman' or 'Jew' is a flight from reality, not a liberation.
principles.rationale: While abstractly all people are human beings, nominalism is inadequate because it ignores the concrete, lived situations and differences that divide humanity into distinct classes with different experiences.
principles.source_quote: To decline to accept such notions as the eternal feminine, the black soul, the Jewish character, is not to deny that Jews, Negroes, women exist today – this denial does not represent a liberation for those concerned, but rather a flight from reality.

principles.statement: Existence is justified only through freely chosen projects that expand into an open future.
principles.rationale: Based on existentialist ethics, a subject achieves liberty through transcendence. Falling back into immanence (stagnation or subjection to given conditions) is a degradation of existence and an absolute evil.
principles.source_quote: Every time transcendence falls back into immanence, stagnation, there is a degradation of existence into the ‘en-sois’ – the brutish life of subjection to given conditions – and of liberty into constraint and contingence.

principles.statement: The public good must be measured by concrete opportunities for individuals, not by 'happiness'.
principles.rationale: Happiness is subjective, unmeasurable, and frequently used by oppressors to justify keeping people in stagnant, subordinate positions by claiming they are 'happy' at rest.
principles.source_quote: we hold that the only public good is that which assures the private good of the citizens; we shall pass judgement on institutions according to their effectiveness in giving concrete opportunities to individuals.

frameworks.name: Existentialist Analysis of Woman's Situation
frameworks.when_to_apply: When attempting to elucidate the condition of women, understand how their independence can be recovered, and determine what directions they should take.
steps: Examine the physiological, psychological, and economic forces often claimed to determine destiny (biology, psychoanalysis, historical materialism).
Trace how the concept of the 'truly feminine' (the Other) was historically fashioned and the consequences this had from the oppressor's (man's) point of view.
Describe the lived world from the perspective of the oppressed (woman's point of view).
Envisage the specific difficulties in their way as they attempt to escape their assigned sphere and aspire to full liberty.
frameworks.source_quote: Hence I shall discuss first of all the light in which woman is viewed by biology, psychoanalysis, and historical materialism. Next I shall try to show exactly how the concept of the ‘truly feminine’ has been fashioned... Then from woman’s point of view I shall describe the world in which women must live

mental_models.name: The One and the Other
mental_models.description: A fundamental duality in human consciousness where a group sets itself up as the essential 'Subject' (The One) by defining another group as the inessential object (The Other).
mental_models.example: In small towns, natives view outsiders as 'strangers'; anti-Semites view Jews as 'different'; and fundamentally, men view themselves as the absolute human type and women as 'the Other'.

mental_models.name: Transcendence vs. Immanence
mental_models.description: An existential lens where 'transcendence' is the active, free expansion of the self through chosen projects, while 'immanence' is stagnation, passivity, and being reduced to a mere object or thing.
mental_models.example: Women are often doomed to immanence by men who stabilize them as objects, overshadowing their fundamental human aspiration for transcendence.

mental_models.name: The Myth of Essence (The Eternal Feminine)
mental_models.description: The false belief in unchangeably fixed entities or Platonic essences (like 'the eternal feminine', 'the black soul', or 'the Jewish character') used by dominant classes to justify the subjugation of a group.
mental_models.example: Anti-feminists argue that women must strive to incarnate the mysterious reality of 'femininity', treating it as a fixed biological or philosophical essence rather than a reaction to a situation.

heuristics: Treat all justifications for women's subordination written by men with suspicion, as they are both judge and party to the case.
Evaluate the situation of individuals in terms of liberty, not in terms of happiness.
When a group is kept in a situation of inferiority, recognize that their current 'inferiority' is a dynamic result of their situation ('to have become'), not a static, natural essence.

signature_quotes.text: He is the Subject, he is the Absolute – she is the Other.
signature_quotes.context: Summarizing how man defines woman relative to himself, denying her autonomy.

signature_quotes.text: A man is in the right in being a man; it is the woman who is in the wrong.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how the masculine is treated as the absolute human norm, while the feminine is treated as a peculiarity or defect.

signature_quotes.text: Otherness is a fundamental category of human thought.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how the duality of the Self and the Other is primordial and not originally attached to the division of the sexes.

signature_quotes.text: Thus it is that no group ever sets itself up as the One without at once setting up the Other over against itself.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the mechanics of in-group/out-group hostility and consciousness.

signature_quotes.text: If woman seems to be the inessential which never becomes the essential, it is because she herself fails to bring about this change.
signature_quotes.context: Analyzing why women, unlike proletarians or racial minorities, have not successfully overthrown their status as the Other.

signature_quotes.text: The drama of woman lies in this conflict between the fundamental aspirations of every subject (ego) – who always regards the self as the essential and the compulsions of a situation in which she is the inessential.
signature_quotes.context: Defining the core existential conflict faced by women.

anti_patterns.description: Evaluating women's situation based on whether they are 'happy'.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Happiness is subjective and unmeasurable. Oppressors frequently use the pretext of happiness to justify condemning people to stagnation and subjection (e.g., claiming a housekeeper is happier than a working-woman).

anti_patterns.description: Adopting nominalism to claim that women are simply 'human beings' and ignoring sex differences.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is an abstract declaration that ignores the concrete reality that humanity is divided into two classes with manifestly different situations. Denying this is a flight from reality, not a liberation.
It functions exactly like the 'equal but separate' Jim Crow laws, serving as a profitable formula for the dominant class that inevitably results in extreme discrimination and segregation.

anti_patterns.description: Believing that women's dependency is the result of a specific historical event that can be easily reversed.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Unlike proletarians or conquered nations, women have always been subordinated to men due to biological facts and a primordial 'Mitsein' (fellowship). They lack a separate history or solidarity of their own, making their cleavage from men impossible.
